key_insights.insight: Knowing what someone is thinking provides a tactical advantage in negotiations, relationships, and career advancement.
key_insights.quote: If you can know what somebody's thinking...when you ask your boss for a raise or when you ask a girl or a guy out on a date. You knowing that is a huge tactical advantage.

key_insights.insight: The number one secret to success is making it about them, not you; focus on what the other person is missing and tailor your approach accordingly.
key_insights.quote: Number one rule...It's not about you. It's always about them. That's been the number one secret to my success.

key_insights.insight: The most interesting person is the most interested person in the room; ask questions that make people think, not autopilot.
key_insights.quote: The most interesting person in a room tends to be the most interested person in the room...Challenge yourself to be the outlier. And think of a question you can ask someone...that throws them out of autopilot.

key_insights.insight: Remembering small details about people and bringing them up later creates a strong positive impression.
key_insights.quote: Do you know how great that feeling is to somebody when you remember things they told you? It's like winning the lottery.

key_insights.insight: Overcoming the fear of rejection is a superpower that enables you to pursue your goals with singular focus.
key_insights.quote: If you can get over that, it is a superpower in life...Because I stopped thinking about it not working.

key_insights.insight: Focusing your attention determines what others will remember; guide their focus to create the desired narrative.
key_insights.quote: It doesn't matter what I do. It matters what people remember. and what's the story they tell others.

key_insights.insight: Vulnerability and opening yourself up in social situations can be a powerful way to break the ice and foster intimacy.
key_insights.quote: I think walking up to somebody has a real power and say, I'm so nervous, I don't know anyone here. Do you know anyone here?

key_insights.insight: Taking notes about every interaction, even seemingly trivial details, provides valuable information that can be used later.
key_insights.quote: Information is power...That information, the longer you hold it, it's a coupon with no expiration date.

key_insights.insight: Memory is a superpower because nobody expects you to have it anymore.
key_insights.quote: I think memory is a superpower because no one expects you to have it anymore.

actionable_advice.advice: Overcome procrastination by tricking your brain.
actionable_advice.how_to: When you're dreading a task, ask yourself how you'll feel about it tomorrow after completing it. Realize it will likely be a non-event and do it now, setting a reminder for tomorrow to acknowledge that feeling.

actionable_advice.advice: Fast track confidence by separating yourself from rejection.
actionable_advice.how_to: Create a separate persona for your professional activities, so rejection applies to the persona and not your core self.

actionable_advice.advice: Use open-ended, positive questions to engage others.
actionable_advice.how_to: Instead of asking "Do you want to see me do magic?", ask "Did you hear what's going on tonight? It's your lucky day."

actionable_advice.advice: Use the "Listen, Repeat, Reply" method to remember names.
actionable_advice.how_to: Actively listen to the name, immediately repeat it to confirm, and reply with a comment or connection to solidify it in your memory.

actionable_advice.advice: Create a calendar entry to make yourself accountable
actionable_advice.how_to: If you are signing up for a 10k, write to 10 people that you know and tell them.

memorable_stories: Spielberg turned the tables and interviewed Perlman
Perlman recalled being told by the CFO that he should be working there, and not at Merrill Lynch
The signed card trick caused audience members to forget that Perlman threw the cards
Remembering a detail about someone's ATM pin code after bumping into them created a memorable experience
contrarian_claims: Inspiration and motivation are garbage; action is the only thing that matters.
While most people aim for amazing, they can forget it, aiming for memorable is better.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Oz Pearlman (Mentalist): This Small Mistake Makes People Dislike You! They Do This, They’re Lying!
